resume:
Devon Carter — Account Executive (Mid-Market SaaS)

Northpeak Software (2021-present) — Account Executive
- Carry a mid-market new-business quota; sell HR/payroll software
- Run full cycle from discovery to close

Clearwave (2019-2021) — Sales Development Rep, then AE (promoted)
- Booked outbound meetings, later carried a small closing quota

Skills: discovery, MEDDIC (familiar), Salesforce, outbound prospecting
Education: B.A. Economics

References available on request.
jd: Northpeak Software seeks a mid-market Account Executive to own net-new revenue for our HR and payroll suite in the 200-2,000 employee segment. You'll run full-cycle deals (avg ACV ~$45k), build and manage your own pipeline, and consistently hit a $900k annual quota. We use MEDDICC and expect rigorous qualification, multi-threaded deals, and accurate forecasting. The strongest candidates can walk through a specific deal in detail: how they found it, how they qualified economic buyer and metrics, the objections they overcame, and the exact path to close with dollar figures and dates.

history.q: Tell me about your current quota and segment at Northpeak.
What was your attainment last year — percentage to quota?
How do you typically build pipeline today?
Walk me through your qualification process. Do you use a framework?
Tell me about your largest closed deal in the last year.
How did you originally source that deal?
Who was the economic buyer, and how did you confirm budget?
What was the toughest objection, and how did you handle it?
What was the ACV and how long was the sales cycle?
What nearly killed the deal at the end?
Give me the single most important thing you did that won that deal.

candidate_last_answer: Honestly, I think the biggest thing was just building a really strong relationship and being there for the customer. I always try to add value and be consultative, you know? It's about understanding their needs and being a trusted advisor. So I just stayed close to them throughout the process and made sure they felt good about us, and eventually it all came together and we got it across the line.

top_question_traits: must-anchor-on: I always try to add value and be consultative

expected-yield: a concrete, specific sequence of selling actions on the named deal — the actual discovery questions, the multi-threading moves, the proof points used — instead of generic 'trusted advisor' platitudes
